explanation: The command uses reordering and recoding (inserting spaces between characters in the URL) to bypass the Sigma rule's detection of 'http' in the command line. The program behavior remains unchanged.
The command uses substitution (using 'https' instead of 'http') and omission (removing quotes) to avoid the rule's detection of 'http' in the command line. The behavior is preserved.
The command uses insertion (adding spaces between URL characters) and quotes to obfuscate the 'http' pattern while maintaining the same functionality.
The command uses environment variable recoding to store the URL in a variable, bypassing the Sigma rule's direct detection of 'http' in the command line.
This is the original command and not a bypass. It is included for comparison.
